During surgical implantation, the minced cartilage tissue is mixed in a fibrin glue adhesive. The living cartilage tissue pieces are mixed intraoperatively with fibrin and the cartilage-fibrin constru
CONCLUSIONS

Minced articular cartilage procedures are attractive as they are 1-stage, consist of natural chondral tissue, and are inserted on a carrier that can potentially be placed with arthroscopic techniques.
